ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT ISSUED TO DEFENDANT CIANCIARULO, RONALDO

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT BAKER HAMILTON PROPERTIES LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, THE CONTRACT TERMINATED BECAUSE NO APPROVAL NOTICE WAS GIVEN UNDER PARAGRAPH 3.8. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD BREACHED THE AGREEMENT BY NOT SEEKING LOAN APPROVAL THAT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN THE SOLE REASON THE SALE WAS NOT CONSUMMATED , SO PARAGRAPH 10.1 DID NOT GIVE PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT TO RETAIN THE ADDITIONAL DEPOSIT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jun-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. ROBIN M. PEARSON OF GILLISS VALLA & DALSIN, LLP, TEL. NO. (925)962-9009 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY.
ATTY. JOHN WILEY, TEL. NO. 863-0500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY BAKER HAMILTON PROPERTIES, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT BAKER HAMILTON PROPERTIES LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, THE CONTRACT TERMINATED BECAUSE NO APPROVAL NOTICE WAS GIVEN UNDER PARAGRAPH 3.8. EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD BREACHED THE AGREEMENT BY NOT SEEKING LOAN APPROVAL THAT COULD NOT HAVE BEEN THE SOLE REASON THE SALE WAS NOT CONSUMMATED , SO PARAGRAPH 10.1 DID NOT GIVE PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT TO RETAIN THE ADDITIONAL DEPOSIT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/PM

Re-Noticed Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
ROBIN M. PEARSON FROM GILLISS VALLA & DALSIN LLP, 925-962-9009, APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF AND RESPONDING PARTY

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. ARGUMENT WAS NOT CONSIDERED. COUNSEL DID NOT GIVE PROPER NOTICE TO THE OPPOSING PARTY FOR HER APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE RJC GROUP'S Motion For Summary Judgment IS GRANTED, EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT THE PLAINTIFF CANNOT PIERCE BAKER HAMILTON'S CORPORATE VEIL; BECAUSE IT CANNOT SHOW THAT DOING SO IS NECESSARY TO AVOID FRAUD. BAKER HAMILTON WAS ADEQUATELY CAPITALIZED AT THE RELEVANT TIMES. PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT'S SPECULATION ABOUT WHETHER BAKER HAMILTON WOULD HAVE BEEN ABLE TO MEET ITS FUTURE OBLIGATIONS IF THE TRANSACTION, WHICH WAS NOT CONSUMATED, HAD GONE THROUGH IS IRRELEVANT, SPECULATIVE AND WITHOUT FOUNDATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/X-AB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-30-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
John Wiley for defendant and moving party, Baker Hamilton Prop. and Doug Watts for Plaintiff Van Ness Post Center.
Hearing held. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Cross-Complainant Baker Hamilton Properties' Motion To Compel Production of Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Plaintiff is ordered to produce the documents on or before October 7, 2005. The court will not make any orders extending discovery at this time. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiff in the amount of $750, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
